title: Using Functions
description: Call built-in functions to make things happen.
content_markdown:
# Using Functions

Functions are little machines you call to make things happen. You'll learn how to invoke them in your code.

title: Using Functions With Inputs
description: Pass inputs (arguments) to functions to control their behaviour.
content_markdown:
# Using Functions With Inputs

Many functions take inputs that change what they do. You'll learn how to pass values into a function when you call it.

title: Using Functions With Return Values
description: Use the values that functions give back to you.
content_markdown:
# Using Functions With Return Values

Some functions hand a value back when they're done. You'll learn how to capture and use that value.

title: Creating Functions
description: Write your own reusable functions.
content_markdown:
# Creating Functions

When built-in functions aren't enough, you can write your own. You'll learn how to define a function from scratch.

title: Creating Functions With Inputs
description: Define functions that accept inputs (parameters).
content_markdown:
# Creating Functions With Inputs

You'll learn how to declare parameters on your own functions so callers can pass values in.

title: Creating Functions With Return Values
description: Make your own functions hand a value back to the caller.
content_markdown:
# Creating Functions With Return Values

You'll learn how to use `return` so your own functions give a result back.

title: Function Composition
description: Combine multiple functions together to solve bigger problems.
content_markdown:
# Function Composition

You'll learn how to use the output of one function as the input of another, building bigger solutions from smaller pieces.

title: Variables
description: Store and reuse values by giving them names.
content_markdown:
# Variables

Variables let you save a value and refer to it by name later.

title: Arithmetic
description: Do maths with numbers and variables.
content_markdown:
# Arithmetic

Use `+`, `-`, `*`, `/` and friends to do calculations.

title: State
description: Update variables to track changing values over time.
content_markdown:
# State

As your program runs, the values stored in variables can change. That changing data is called state.

title: Strings
description: Work with text in your programs.
content_markdown:
# Strings

Strings are pieces of text. You'll learn how to create and use them.

title: String Concatenation
description: Join strings together with `+`.
content_markdown:
# String Concatenation

You'll learn how to glue strings together end to end.

title: String Templates
description: Build strings by interpolating values into a template.
content_markdown:
# String Templates

Templates let you slot variables and expressions into a string without lots of `+`s.

title: String Indexing
description: Access individual characters by position.
content_markdown:
# String Indexing

Each character in a string has a position. You'll learn how to grab the one you want.

title: String Iteration
description: Loop through a string one character at a time.
content_markdown:
# String Iteration

You'll learn how to walk through a string character by character.

title: Colors
description: Specify and use colours in your drawings.
content_markdown:
# Colors

You'll learn how to pick and use colours when drawing.

title: HSL Colors
description: Describe colours by hue, saturation and lightness.
content_markdown:
# HSL Colors

HSL is one way to specify a colour by its hue, saturation, and lightness.

title: RGB Colors
description: Describe colours by mixing red, green and blue.
content_markdown:
# RGB Colors

RGB is another way to specify a colour, by mixing red, green, and blue.

title: Loops
description: Repeat actions in your code without rewriting them.
content_markdown:
# Loops

Loops let you do the same thing many times without repeating yourself.

title: Repeat Loop
description: Repeat a block of code a fixed number of times.
content_markdown:
# Repeat Loop

The repeat loop runs the same block a set number of times.

title: Nested Loops
description: Put loops inside other loops.
content_markdown:
# Nested Loops

A loop inside another loop is a nested loop, useful for grids and 2D problems.

title: Repeat While
description: Repeat code until a condition stops being true.
content_markdown:
# Repeat While

Use repeat-while when you don't know in advance how many times to loop.

title: For Loops
description: Loop with a counter, with full control over start, stop and step.
content_markdown:
# For Loops

For loops give you fine control over how a counter advances each iteration.

title: Break
description: Exit a loop early.
content_markdown:
# Break

`break` stops a loop immediately, even if the condition is still true.

title: Continue
description: Skip the rest of the current loop iteration and move to the next.
content_markdown:
# Continue

`continue` skips the rest of this iteration and jumps to the next one.

title: Conditionals
description: Make decisions in code based on whether something is true.
content_markdown:
# Conditionals

Conditionals let your program take different paths depending on the situation.

title: If Statements
description: Run code only when a condition is true.
content_markdown:
# If Statements

`if` runs a block of code only when its condition is true.

title: Else
description: Run alternative code when an `if` condition is false.
content_markdown:
# Else

`else` runs when the `if` condition wasn't true.

title: Else If
description: Chain multiple conditions in order.
content_markdown:
# Else If

`else if` lets you check additional conditions in sequence.

title: Logical And
description: Combine conditions that must all be true.
content_markdown:
# Logical And

`and` is true only when both sides are true.

title: Logical Or
description: Combine conditions where at least one must be true.
content_markdown:
# Logical Or

`or` is true when either side is true.

title: Logical Not
description: Flip a condition's truth value.
content_markdown:
# Logical Not

`not` turns true into false and false into true.

title: Modulo
description: Get the remainder of a division.
content_markdown:
# Modulo

The modulo operator gives you what's left over after dividing.

title: Using If and State
description: Combining if statements with variables that change over time so your program reacts and behaves differently as it runs.
content_markdown:
# Conditionals and State

Mixing conditionals with state lets your program respond differently as values change.

title: Scope
description: Understand where variables exist and where they don't.
content_markdown:
# Scope

Variables defined inside a block only exist inside that block.

title: Random
description: Add randomness to your programs.
content_markdown:
# Random

Random values let your programs behave differently each time they run.

title: Animation
description: Make things move on screen.
content_markdown:
# Animation

Animation is what happens when you draw, change something, and redraw, repeatedly.

title: Scenarios
description: Run your code against different starting conditions.
content_markdown:
# Scenarios

Scenarios let you verify your code works in lots of different situations, not just one.

title: Methods
description: Call functions that come attached to a value.
content_markdown:
# Methods

Methods are functions that belong to a value, like `.length` or `.toUpperCase()`.

title: Properties
description: Access named values attached to a thing.
content_markdown:
# Properties

Properties are named values that hang off another value.

title: Arrays
description: Store an ordered collection of values.
content_markdown:
# Arrays

Arrays let you store and work with lists of values.

title: Building Arrays
description: Build up an array piece by piece, often by appending in a loop.
content_markdown:
# Building Arrays

Start with an empty array and grow it as you go.

title: Dictionaries
description: Store values under named keys.
content_markdown:
# Dictionaries

Dictionaries (also called maps) store data under named keys.

title: Updating Dictionaries
description: Add, change and remove keys in a dictionary.
content_markdown:
# Updating Dictionaries

Dictionaries aren't fixed once created — you can add new keys, change values, and remove entries.
